violations.finding: Newly introduced direct python3 invocation: `python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py` without venv activation. Added in uncommitted working-tree diff for this cycle.
violations.recommendation: Either use `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py ...` or codify an explicit stdlib-only exception in Standard 3 before claiming clean. The prose note at lines 208-210 is an explanation, not a formal standard exemption.

violations.finding: Newly introduced direct python3 invocation: second occurrence of `python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py` (--auto variant) without venv activation.
violations.recommendation: Same as commands/push.md:184 — use venv activation or codify a formal stdlib exception in the standard.

violations.finding: Newly introduced direct python3 invocation in working-tree diff: `python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID`. Prior committed version said 'The orchestrator writes the aggregate inline via jq or python3 in a single Bash call' (generic prose). Current working-tree version introduces a concrete python3 invocation without venv activation.
violations.recommendation: Use `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id "$TASK_ID"` or document a formal stdlib-only exception in Standard 3.

violations.finding: Newly introduced direct python3 invocation in working-tree diff: `python3 scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py --task-id $TASK_ID` in example comment.
violations.recommendation: Align with Standard 3: use `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py` in both prose and example.

violations.finding: Chinese text in code comment: `#   /allow --tool rm 删冗余文件             -> literal "rm" + comment`. Standard 6 requires unconditional full-scan of hooks regardless of --changed-files scope.
violations.recommendation: Translate to English: `#   /allow --tool rm remove-redundant-files   -> literal "rm" + comment`. Note: this is a pre-existing violation introduced in commit f033d6d — line 45 is outside this cycle's diff hunks (which start at lines 85+ and 210+). Per AC-2.6(b) soft fallback: overlap-only proxy used; close.md should treat this as ADVISORY (pre-existing, not introduced by this cycle).

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 7 issues. 4 were applied (Standard 3 findings for push.md and dev.md direct python3 calls). 3 were rejected: Standard 1/8 on push.md code block (it is a CLI invocation example, not implementation logic); Standard 2/9 on execute-push.py constants (infrastructure paths matching push.sh convention); dangling-reference on aggregate-dev-report.py (file exists on disk; Standard 10 checks disk existence, not git tracking). Standard 6 Chinese text finding was already captured independently.

findings.issue: Standard 3 miss: commands/push.md:184,191 direct python3 invocations without venv
findings.proposed_fix: Replace both with source venv invocations or codify stdlib exception in Standard 3
findings.rationale: Git diff confirms these lines are newly introduced in the working-tree diff. Standard 3 has no documented stdlib-only exemption. The prose note at 208-210 is explanatory only.

findings.issue: Standard 1/8 miss: commands/push.md:182-197 contains executable multi-line bash block
findings.proposed_fix: Replace fenced block with concise prose
findings.rationale: The bash block is a CLI invocation example showing how to call execute-push.py. Standard 1 explicitly says 'not examples' are violations. This is an illustrative example, not implementation logic. No violation.

findings.issue: Standard 3 miss: commands/dev.md:709,747 direct python3 aggregate-dev-report.py invocations
findings.proposed_fix: Use source venv && python, or codify stdlib exception
findings.rationale: Git diff confirms these are newly introduced in the working-tree diff (prior committed version used different prose). Standard 3 requires venv activation.

findings.issue: Standard 2/9 likely miss: scripts/execute-push.py:34,37 hardcoded sentinel base and push.sh path
findings.proposed_fix: Add --sentinel-base and --push-sh CLI parameters with env-var defaults
findings.rationale: These module-level constants mirror push.sh's own hardcoded path (push.sh:97). They represent an infrastructure convention, not user-configurable values. Standard 2 exempts 'constants that genuinely never change'. The push.sh path is tilde-expanded at runtime. No parameterization is warranted.

findings.issue: Standard 6 miss: hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:45 Chinese text without advisory finding
findings.proposed_fix: Translate to English and surface as advisory
findings.rationale: Standard 6 requires unconditional scan. Finding surfaced as advisory (pre-existing, outside this cycle's diff hunks).

findings.issue: Standard 3 policy gap: hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh direct python3 invocations with no formal hook exception
findings.proposed_fix: Refactor into venv Python helper or formally amend Standard 3 to exempt Claude hook scripts
findings.rationale: Hook files run in harness process context where no venv is available. All peer hook files (pretool-bash-safety.sh, etc.) use the same pattern. This is an established project convention, not a new deviation. OBSERVATION_ONLY — tracking for potential Standard 3 amendment.

findings.issue: Dangling coverage risk: commands/dev.md references scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py which is currently untracked
findings.proposed_fix: Include aggregate-dev-report.py in the changed-files audit set and commit it
findings.rationale: Standard 10 checks that referenced scripts exist on disk. aggregate-dev-report.py exists at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/aggregate-dev-report.py. The untracked (uncommitted) status is a git hygiene concern, not a style standard violation. OBSERVATION_ONLY.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied 4 codex findings as Standard 3 violations (push.md:184, push.md:191, dev.md:709, dev.md:747). Rejected Standard 1/8 finding (CLI example, not implementation). Rejected Standard 2/9 finding (infrastructure constants). Logged two observations (hook exception gap, untracked script). Standard 6 advisory was already independently captured.
